reviews.review_description: 𝑇ℎ𝑖𝑠 𝑎𝑝𝑝 𝑖𝑠 𝑏𝑦 𝑓𝑎𝑟 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑏𝑒𝑠𝑡 𝑡𝑟𝑖𝑝𝑝𝑦, 𝑣𝑖𝑠𝑢𝑎𝑙𝑖𝑧𝑒𝑟 𝐼'𝑣𝑒 𝑒𝑣𝑟 ℎ𝑎𝑑. 𝐼'𝑚 𝑠𝑜 𝑔𝑙𝑎𝑑 𝐼 𝑝𝑢𝑟𝑐ℎ𝑎𝑠𝑒𝑑 𝑖𝑡. 𝐼'𝑣𝑒 𝑡𝑟𝑖𝑒𝑑 𝑜𝑡ℎ𝑟 𝑠𝑖𝑚𝑖𝑙𝑎𝑟 𝑎𝑝𝑝𝑠 𝑐𝑎𝑙𝑙𝑑 𝑡𝑟𝑖𝑝𝑝 𝑛 𝑅𝑒𝑎𝑙𝑚𝑠 𝑜𝑓 𝑓𝑙𝑜𝑤... 𝐵𝑢𝑡 𝑡ℎ𝑖𝑠 𝑜𝑛𝑒 𝑏𝑒𝑎𝑡𝑠 𝑎𝑙𝑙 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑟𝑒𝑠𝑡, 𝐼 𝑒𝑣𝑛 𝑙𝑢𝑣 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑚𝑢𝑠𝑖𝑐 𝑛 𝐼 𝑗𝑢𝑠 𝑟𝑒𝑎𝑙𝑖𝑧𝑒𝑑 𝑎 𝑙𝑖𝑙 𝑏𝑖𝑡 𝑎𝑔𝑜 𝑡ℎ𝑎𝑡 𝑖𝑓 𝑢 𝑡𝑢𝑟𝑛 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑜𝑝𝑝𝑜𝑠𝑖𝑡𝑒 𝑤𝑎𝑦 𝑡ℎ𝑒𝑛 𝑒𝑣𝑟𝑦𝑡ℎ𝑛 𝑖𝑠 𝑔𝑜𝑖𝑛 𝑖𝑛𝑠𝑡𝑒𝑎𝑑 𝑜𝑓 𝑐𝑜𝑚𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑡𝑜𝑤𝑎𝑟𝑑 𝑢. 5⭐, 𝑏𝑢𝑡 𝐼'𝑑 𝑔𝑖𝑣 𝑖𝑡 10 𝑖𝑓 𝐼 𝑐𝑜𝑢𝑙𝑑. 𝑃𝑙𝑧 𝑘𝑒𝑒𝑝 𝑖𝑚𝑝𝑟𝑜𝑣𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑛 𝑎𝑑𝑑𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑛𝑒𝑤 𝑣𝑖𝑠𝑢𝑎𝑙 𝑒𝑓𝑓𝑒𝑐𝑡𝑠 𝑛 𝑚𝑜𝑟 𝑘𝑖𝑐𝑘 𝑎𝑧𝑧 𝑚𝑢𝑠𝑖𝑐. 𝐼 𝐿𝑈𝑉 𝐼𝑇!!!
I prefer using it on Quest 2 instead of Quest 3, I think it has more of a 3D feel there.

reviews.review_title: Visuals get repetitive after a while.
reviews.review_description:
I'd pay for downloadable packs of additional visualizations. If the dev team just took a look at the hundreds of Milkdrop visualizations from back in the day and emulated even half of them, they could rake in more money easily.

As always, Spotify or other streaming source integration is the holy grail here. Please look at allowing integration of other streaming music sources.

I'm giving this two stars only because the visuals are pretty good.  Not great, but good.  That being said, it's not a very good application.  The interface is clumsy and not in any way intuitive.  They claim it can be used with DLNA streaming, yet there is nothing in the menu that shows how to do it.  I was also hoping they would include the ability to use services such as Spotify, so I found it disappointing that you cannot.  The music provided with the app is not to my liking. either.  It didn't cost much overall, but I'm likely going to ask for a refund anyway.  It doesn't feel like something I'd be using regularly.

Firstly, I'd like to say i'm astonished at the complete lack of these sorts of visualizer apps, much less ones with as basic of a function as being able to search folders. This app was very close to the thing I was looking for when it came to the desire for a VR visualizer, though after some time with it there are things to iron out and features I'm hoping that are considered in an update to make this a genuinely great app.

-On the Quest 3, it's pretty noticeable and slightly distracting how low resolution all the effects can be seen through the pancake lenses over the fresnels of the 3S and 2, the assets could use some upscaling for the new system.

-An option to have two visual effects active at once, something like having skybox-esque ones such as the constellations or kaleidoscopes act independently from effects directly in front of you, if they were to be broken into categories. Possibly new options to have something other than a black void that's active during most effects. On the note of the kaleidoscope effect though, the third(?) one in particular can be awfully strainful to look at when it attempts to match songs with high tempo, that seriously needs some tweaking.

-The option to customize visualizers to some extent, from the colors with the choice to have them cycle through variants, to extra flourishes like particles and ghosting "trail" effects, open the door to fun experimentation that keeps things fresh.

-ability to create playlists with the option to set visualizers on a per-playlist basis.

-Generally would enjoy seeing new visualizers being added that further push a stronger sense of artistic vision, some of the current ones can come off a bit more on the basic side of things.

I see a lot of potential here and appreciate the appeal to such a niche interest, I can only hope though that any major development for this app isn't entirely off the table. This is one I want to come back and unwind to often and recommend to others.

definitely recommend! I was having trouble with loading songs onto it for a while, but was able to get in touch with the dev and he helped me sort out my file issues quickly. great visualizer and definitely worth it.

High quality formats are not supported (FLACs and M4A AAC or ALAC). So, I'd bear with MP3 320kbs or lossless WAVs right? Wrong! They sound awful in this app! It seems to playback at 16bits or something, not Hi-res. It doesn't play back your music at their native stream rate. My UPNP server stream also didn't work as well. The Quest natively supports LDAC which is 24bit/96khz audio stream via a compatible bluetooth DAC, so there is no excuse for this glorified music player to play music this bad. It's a shame, I feel as if this has so much potential, but this just feels like a quick cash grab. But I'll give the developer credit for the cool VFX though.

I can’t get it to import my music. I tried 4 songs and it just says it’s verifying   Will only play what is in the list or it’s channels 
Maybe I’m not understanding the instructions or I’m missing something

While I understand Spotify integration is the holy grail, perhaps allowing processing from the microphone would be a good bridge in the meantime. The “Tunr” app on the Apple app store comes to mind. They have Spotify access built in, but also allow to use microphone as the source.
Thank you for this experience. Can't wait for more effects! How about SoundCloud or YouTube audio integration?

The visuals are impressive, and the sort of thing I've been looking for. However it's all a bit dizzying if you aren't careful. I think it would be nice if I could connect this to my Spotify or something. Also for as trippy as the visual effects are I would like to see the music more visualized in the future. Not that watching spinning tunnels of rainbow psychidelic wonderment isn't nice, I just wish that it melded with the music more. Right now it feels more like a windows screen saver, and less like a visualizer.

This is a great VR music visualizer that has a lot of options - such as modes for reclining, scene length, 120Hz mode, etc.  It's responsive and psychedelic. I love how each scene fades into the next.  Listening to Shpongle in this is total bliss. I just wish it had a few more variations.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review, Zululord! More effects are on the way.

reviews.review_description: Evryway is easy to use and provides stimulating visualization for music. I have already used the app to create a video with my original music. Thanx.

reviews.review_title: awesome..add ur fav tracks and relax
reviews.review_description: wish new effects like chadni plates or harmonic frequencies will be incorporated

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! More effects are on the way.

reviews.review_title: Really cool app and it keeps improving
reviews.review_description: Awesome package of visuals that adapt and react to music. I like this app a lot and it keeps improving. Thank you devs for considering user feedback and delivering updates that add value to the experience.

Greetings, former sound designer/producer turned Computer Science student here.

Having grown up with music software such as Winamp with its Milkdrop visualizer and having always been fascinated by audiovisual underground art by the demoscene, one of the first things I thought of after getting my Oculus Quest is: I wonder how a visualizer like Milkdrop would look like in VR if you render a 360/180 scene with stereoscopy/dual camera...

This application actually makes my desire come true, easily deserves 5 stars and I will be promoting it on communities. The visuals are captivating and hearthis.at has enough music and dj sets to keep you busy for a greater timespan than a whole lifetime 😁

Great experience, 5* - well deserved

A couple of feature suggestions and thanks to the dev:

1. A one hand mode (only right controller), or mapping customization. Especially a "next track" button.
2. As an actual hearthis artist account owner I would appreciate a user search function (to search for a username) and ideally a bookmark/favorites feature...
3. A clear cache button for hearthis if none exists.
4. I wish we could also start the streams directly instead of having to wait for longer mixes to get fully cached. I mean, hearthis uses mp3 files, correct? If loaded sequentially partial/incomplete playback would not be an issue :)
5. Could we also have Soundcloud integration? I don't mean the new Spotify-like premium service they have, but regular user uploads.

And one last plea:

Take inspiration from the demoscene when creating visuals if you want... such as the demo called Chaos Theory by the group called Conspiracy. It can be seen here: https://youtu.be/ZfuierUvx1A
Seeing what these assembly 'Gods' can fit into a 64KB binary makes me wanna see what the limits of a modern engine are...

Thanks in advance for reading this and thank you for your hard work on this. The visuals are breathtaking and I am having more fun with this than I have with most actual games on the headset.

developer_response.body: Hey ScienceNonfiction, thanks for the great feedback! I've added your requests to my backlog, keep an eye on future updates!

reviews.review_description:
pf - dark side of the moon + visualiser

I expect immersive concerts to become more of a thing. In the meantime this visualizer is worth getting.
Great music app. Some of the effects are trippy though so u may want to disable some of them.

This app is great for relaxing, after heavy beatsabering, etc. My $5 were well spent.

An improvement I would like to see in the future: Make it easier / possible to loop the same track and the same visualization over and over again. Most of the time, this is what I need for relaxing.

Another suggestion: Some of the visualizations have bright lines on black background, and look terrible due to lack of antialiasing. These visualizations hurt my eyes and I switch away immediately whenever I see them. Please add antialiasing if possible, and/or add a glow / blur / convolution / whatever effect around the lines to make them look less jagged.

This is an AMAZING app. The visualizations are incredible in VR. There is an excellent variety of them and some are seriously mesmerizing. I had my mouth open. I kid you not, one of the best visual apps I've seen and the visuals are in tune with the music. There is a great selection of music and mixes that is integrated into the "hear this" area of the app so you have many awesome music options in all different genres. I cannot recommend this app enough. There are literally dozens of visualizations to keep things fresh and many of them are just WOW. This needs to come out on the actual store and be supported by more people as it is FANTASTIC. A hidden gem I have stumbled upon.
